description: This map illustrates the geographic distribution of Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) Urban Atlas mapping zones across Metropolitan France, with a small portion of Réunion visible in the bottom right. The base layer is a grey-scale satellite image of France, showing general terrain. Superimposed on this base, numerous distinct urban agglomerations are highlighted. Each of these agglomerations is delineated by a red outline, within which detailed land cover mapping is visible. The land cover within these outlined areas is represented by colours, predominantly shades of green (indicating vegetated areas like green urban areas, forests, or grasslands), with some lighter yellow/beige and red-brown elements (likely indicating built-up or agricultural areas). The context suggests these detailed areas are part of the Urban Atlas 2021 dataset, focusing on differentiating green urban areas. The mapped zones are spread throughout France, clustering around major cities, with concentrations notable in northern, eastern, and southeastern regions. No scale bar, compass, legend, or explicit year is visible on the map itself.
